review_text: Well I don’t know why I can’t seem to get responses back in a “respectful client compassionate way” from Aliya the paralegal for Annie Surbaugh. I send email after email without responses by email back or phone calls. Then my last email 11/18/2022 to Annie Surbaugh comes back that she is out of the office until 11/28/2022. Now I must wait 10 days more for a response back because apparently Aliya Larson(paralegal) is out too?? Seriously a law firm can close for 10 days no warning to clients by email who are waiting for responses? VERY UNPROFESSIONAL and I will be happy when I am done with this firm!

I have had to do all the legwork of my disability appeal since original submission in December. Crickets from my legal aide. Talked to her once and she had no idea what was going on. When my great rep, Danielle, left I was also left out. A shame too. I had high hopes :-(

My LTD appeal took a long time. I believe Fields Law did not do anything I could not have done on my own  I was still very involved with collecting medical  records and following up with my doctors.  I do have a complicated medical history with 9 doctors at 3 large university hospitals . Medical Records delays were the reason for my LTD case to be closed resulting in an appeal. Ultimately the the appeal was approved. All things considered the fee seemed to be high for what they did.
Sued for 120k settlement was 20k not satisfied with their efforts

I was disappointed in the entire process. I was assigned to 3 different attorney's. Each time a little information and understanding of my case was lost. I feel my settlement amount suffered due to this issue. After 2 years, I received a final settlement offer. I was advised if I didn't accept it and was awarded benefits through the court process, the insurance company can stop paying benefits at any time or possibly not be awarded any benefits. Then the process of proving disability begins all over again... the thought of that vicious circle just wasn't worth it. The entire staff is really nicr,  professional & qualified.
I'm not sure if they have a large turnover issue resulting in my unfortunate situation. It would have been great if someone keep me in the loop. I kept calling & sending messages without replies. I finally I went through the main number and was told my attorney was no longer working there. I received a call after my case was reassigned to new attorney. He was well versed in my case and very positive. Then towards the end of the process, I received a call from the final attorney to tell me he was now handling my case. He was totally uninformed of the details of my case.

My original attorney felt I had a strong enough case to go all the way to litigation. She left the practice and my case was handed off to another attorney who was only interested in settling the case and getting it off his desk. He pushed for me to accept a settlement worth a third of what I was entitled to. I stupidly agreed. It is done and in the past now. Nothing more I can do.

I think there could have been more communication from lawyer at times I had to ask what’s going on with the case.
Lawyers assistant a lot of times did not respond to me especially regarding ambulance bill we should not have had to pay and was assured it would be taken care of instead was taken out of our taxes! Still not satisfied with that outcome.
At beginning of case I was told we were looking at a six figure settlement and as time went on I was lucky to get a lot less for being out of work for almost two years. I kind of felt mislead.
I felt at times that my case wasn’t important or a waste of time for the lawyer. I felt lawyer should have let me know that someone else was going to attend a second interview with opposing council.
With that said
Lawyer was very nice in person. This is my second time with this firm.

Fields law firm was really helpful. The legal team kept in close contact with me, from start to finish. They were very knowledgeable about disability claims in general,  and knew what to do through every step of my case. Having someone go through the long legal process of a disability case with you, like they did was invaluable.
owner_response: Glad to know that you had a great experience.

review_text:
I wasn’t planning to leave a review, but after today I’m done. I hired Hoglund & Mrozik, P.L.L.C. for a criminal matter. At first, everything seemed solid. Unfortunately, that didn’t last.

I was given guidance that ultimately cost me over $1,000 and wasn’t fully explained—especially how certain actions would affect me later. My case was also delayed nearly five months, only to end in a plea deal that likely could have happened much earlier.

I was prepared to let all of that go. What pushed me over the edge was being contacted six months after my case was settled and told I still owed money. Thankfully, I keep records. After I provided receipts, they reviewed it again and confirmed I had, in fact, paid in full.

Make of that what you will.

Paid $2400 cash to retain Robert Hoglund plus $348 filing fee just to end up in bankruptcy court to pay the trustee another $2500. I didn’t do my homework and suffered in the end. I initially called for a consultation and before I knew it I was signing papers. The staff (paralegals) Erica Olson, Elvin Rengifo, and Abbi Kutz are rude, don’t return calls, and only want to respond through emails at their convenience. I emailed Robert Hoglund not long before my court date and said how I was going to leave a review and he ended up sending Doug Weimerskirich to represent me after he assured me that he’d be in court with me. The entire process was a huge headache that was costly. When you retain them, they work for you, you don’t work for them. I somehow spent a lot of time sending in bank statements, check stubs, and other documents that I could have done had I filed on my own. I DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS LAW FIRM. Call around and get free consultations before you pay this company. YOU’VE BEEN WARNED.

If I could go after them for misrepresentation I definitely would. Shortly after becoming my attorney and having a court date there was a massive wait. I had to call the disability office in the cities and see what is going on because my attorney would always say "we are waiting for them to make a decision." What ended up happening is they were waiting for an income statement that wasn't coming for 6 months. I had to tell my attorney when our next court date was more than once because they wouldn't keep checking on my case. I also was on the phone with the judge's assistant trying to get things moving forward because my attorney was never checking on my case to try and move things forward. I was approved for disability but they stopped working when they got paid. I tried multiple times to speak to an attorney about my back pay but I was belittled by multiple attorneys saying "I should be grateful for them getting me approved." When I explained the situation they simply told me "everything looks and sounds correct." My back pay was incorrect and after a year of them getting phone calls from me asking for updates they said "nothing can be done and everything is correct as far as your back pay." I then called and asked to speak to the brand partner and left a message but I only received a letter from the brand partner saying "nothing can be done about your back pay" so I had to make a few calls. After one phone call I was able to get my back pay figured out and the disability office said that there was an error and they were going to correct it. If I didn't do the leg work that my attorney never did I wouldn't be receiving the correct amount because my attorney wasn't going to get paid anymore money because they already maxed out their pay. Stay away from Hoglund Law.

Would not recommend to anyone! SS helped me get out! This law firm did NOT turn in any medical records and is horrible with communication, went 2 1/2 years to lose all because my medical records were not turned in. SS reached out to me which never happens. I was so thankful, now I know what my next step is! This has not only hurt me but hurt my family. I truly believe this has been a money scam to them and they just truly don't care about their customers!!! I even offered to get my medical records and they said they had, but didn't!!! I hope and pray no one has to go through what my family has been put through.
Communication was bad, didn’t solve anything with my case, only wasted a year of my time and 2k. Still lost my license and had to pay 1500+ to the courts/DMV for a misdemeanor.

Called to get help for the person on the phone to get every little detail of my personal life and business for 45 mins to be emailed while on the phone saying they won't work with me. But won't explain why and give you the run around. Stay away from this place!

Lawyers and associate are not caring at all. Waited 2 years and only heard from them 2 or 3 times in the 2 years and only heard from the actual attorney a day before the hearing. I get that they are busy but they didn’t even get all my health information that they were supposed to. I don’t believe this is normal for an attorney. I had to call numerous times and they still haven’t returned my call after my case. They never answered the questions I had either or explained the case to me well enough considering my disability. Now I’m left with no way to survive without this support. Very frustrating, they are not helpful or professional. I don’t recommend this place.

Hoglund attorneys would not help because it wasn't worth the money they would collect. I would get more money (SSI) than retirement pay I'm receiving now. Don't let them fool you, they want only the big and quick (open and shut) cases that make the most profit! Secretary Candice will act like they're caring and can make your settlement quick and big and easy, as they ask every single fact in your life, including your entire social security number, which is unacceptable. I gave only the last four numbers and was ok to proceed. I had two letters from neurosurgeon and orthopedic doctors stating that I was 100% disabled. Still they wouldn't take the case. So come to your own conclusion, they are in it only for profit.

Horrible appointment set up, phone tag, and no one seemed to know I had an appointment set up. Had to repeat the same information many times to many different people only to find out they're not even licensed in my state. Absolute joke.

It’s crazy how someone’s life had been torn apart because of my injury, lost income, my potential has diminished, and the strain on my relationships. How bad does someone have to be to get any help? And because I can’t make enough now has created so much debt. I’m not a lazy person or looking for any handouts, but thanks for the support.

Completely disappointed – do not trust this firm with your disability case.

I am beyond frustrated and disappointed with this law firm, and I feel compelled to warn others. Not only did they fail me during my appeal, but they also handled my original Social Security disability application, which was denied. Now I’m left in an even worse situation than before, struggling to get the help I need.

After my initial denial, I reached out to the firm for assistance with the appeal, hoping they’d offer the expertise I needed. Instead, the firm canceled or didn’t show up for multiple calls after I informed them about the denial. It was like they didn’t care, and I was just left hanging.

Eventually, a paralegal named Juliana was supposed to follow up with me, but instead I got an outsourced contractor from Mexico who was completely unprepared and unfamiliar with my case. I have cognitive impairments, and the communication barrier made it impossible to get the correct information across. Not only could she not understand what I was saying, but she couldn’t even spell basic details correctly.

By the end of the call, it became clear that this person wasn’t offering any real legal advice—she was just filling out an appeal form on the Social Security website. This person had access to all of my information, including my social security number, and was working from her home in a small town in Mexico, which raised data security concerns. The attorney I hired has not spoken to me. They never reviewed my case, never offered insight into why my initial application was denied, and didn’t suggest any ways to strengthen the appeal. Just today I contacted Carrie, my case manager, to ask questions about an appointment that was scheduled by social security and she was unable to answer what the tests listed on the appointment sheet were.

This firm handled my original disability application, and I trusted them to do everything they could to help me. Instead, they’ve shown nothing but neglect and unprofessionalism. My family and I depend on these benefits to survive, and it feels like they’re not taking that seriously at all. The lack of communication, the failure to provide real legal counsel, and the overall disregard for my case is infuriating.

I would strongly advise anyone seeking Social Security disability assistance to avoid this firm at all costs. Save yourself the stress, time, and heartache.

This law firm has a concerning track record of turning down potential clients without conducting a proper evaluation of their cases. Specifically, they often do not take the time to thoroughly review medical records and pertinent documentation when asked to represent individuals facing social security issues. This apparent disregard for essential information not only undermines the clients' chances of receiving the assistance they need, but it also raises questions about the firm's commitment to providing comprehensive legal support.
